faithfulness.justification: Every factual claim in the tutor's answer is directly supported by the retrieved chunks. The definitions of feed-forward networks (no cycles, outputs not fed back) and recurrent networks (feedback connections, closed loops) are all grounded in chunk 3650b8da-e54b-4d06-84a1-b44ff3b0fb2e.

answer_relevancy.justification: The answer directly and completely addresses the question by clearly defining both feed-forward and recurrent neural networks and explicitly contrasting their key distinguishing features (acyclic vs. feedback loops).
evidence: Feed-forward: acyclic, information moves only forward
Recurrent: has feedback/loops, allowing previous outputs to influence later computation
outputs from a node are not fed back into the same layer or any earlier layer
node outputs can be directed back as inputs to the same layer or a previous layer

answer_correctness.justification: The tutor's answer covers all key facts from the ground truth: feed-forward networks have no node outputs used as inputs to the same or preceding layers (no cycles), while recurrent networks have closed loops with feedback connections. The answer is factually consistent with no contradictions.
evidence: Ground truth: 'no node output is used as an input to a node in the same layer or in a preceding layer' vs Tutor: 'outputs from a node are not fed back into the same layer or any earlier layer'
Ground truth: 'recurrent network features closed loops' vs Tutor: 'has feedback connections... creates closed loops (recurrence)'

context_precision.justification: Nearly all chunks are relevant to distinguishing feed-forward from recurrent networks. Chunk 3650b8da directly defines both types, chunk 946597e6 provides structural examples, and chunks 0eb9fe51 and a6de2824 describe feed-forward architectures. Only chunks fd02aa69, a5a8f96e, bb78f7dc, and c203c17e provide general ANN background without directly addressing the structural distinction.

context_recall.justification: All key facts from the ground truth are present in the retrieved chunks. Chunk 3650b8da explicitly defines feed-forward networks as having no node output as input to the same or preceding layer, and defines feedback/recurrent networks as having closed loops with outputs directed back to same or preceding layers.
evidence: An ANN is called *feed-forward network* if no node output is an input to a node in the same layer or in a preceding layer
When node outputs can be directed back as inputs to a node in the same (or a preceding) layer, it is a *feedback network*
Feedback networks that have closed loops are called *recurrent networks*
